title: Maple Cricket League — a streetwear cricket league: vectorize the logo system, build the sublimation/screen-print kit artwork, and grade the brand photography into a production-ready package
grounding_note: Faithful to the real Upwork Maple Cricket League brief. Verbatim-anchored elements: the league identity/positioning, the diaspora/GTA audience, the Rastah/OVO/Aimé Leon Dore/IPL aesthetic references, the "concept fully developed, need execution" framing, and the explicit line items for a production-ready logo system (crest/monogram/hat badge), home+away sublimation kit print files, and organized/named/production-ready delivery. Reasonably enriched (and flagged as such in the full grounding_note field): scoping the benchmark to ONLY the Adobe-connector-doable production-art thread and dropping the out-of-scope deliverables (Webflow/Squarespace site, written brand copy, Canva/Figma Instagram templates, the prose brand-guidelines document), plus specifying the five concrete client-supplied input assets and the one authored .ai master required so render_vector has a genuine file to export. No unrelated deliverable is invented.
one_line_ask: Turn Maple Cricket League's already-designed raster logo sketches and brand photos into a production-ready set of named files: clean vector marks, a halftone sublimation/screen-print kit treatment, cohesive streetwear color-grade, a cut-out product shot, and a wide banner reframed from the graded hero.
full_brief:
Maple Cricket League (Backyard Cricket League Canada) is a streetwear-forward cricket league for the South Asian and Caribbean diaspora in the GTA, with an aesthetic in the lane of Rastah, OVO, Aimé Leon Dore, and IPL. The concept is fully developed; the client needs execution and production-ready, organized, named files.

You are the creative director executing the Adobe production-art thread. The client supplies: (a) a flat raster scan/mockup of the full logo system art (crest + monogram + hat badge laid out together) on a plain background; (b) an authored Illustrator master file (.ai) of the same three marks set up on three artboards, ready to export; (c) a hero brand photo of a street cricket scene shot in mixed/overcast GTA light; (d) a studio-style photo of a folded home jersey on a plain seamless backdrop for the e-commerce/lookbook cut-out; (e) a flat photo of the away-kit chest crest graphic to be turned into a single-color screen-print/sublimation separation.

Produce, by chaining Adobe connector tools: (1) a clean, scalable VECTOR of the logo system from the raster scan (raster→SVG), then an exported vector master from the supplied authored .ai for the manufacturer; (2) a one-color HALFTONE screen-print / sublimation treatment of the away-kit crest, straightened, cropped to the print bounds, and converted to a print-ready halftone separation; (3) a cohesive streetwear COLOR GRADE of the hero brand photo (auto-tone baseline → exposure/contrast → split-tone-style monochromatic warmth → grain) so the photography matches the OVO/ALD reference; (4) a transparent-background PRODUCT CUT-OUT of the folded jersey for the lookbook; (5) a wide BANNER crop of the graded hero for the site/social header, reframed and resized to the wide banner dimensions from the existing frame (cropped/resized to the target ratio, using the room already in the shot). Deliver every export as an organized, clearly named, production-ready file.

Hard rules: stay production-faithful — no inventing new logo concepts, no from-scratch art, no text-to-image. The vector export must come from the genuine authored .ai the client supplies. Every step after each asset load must consume a prior step's output.

inputs.name: logo_system_raster.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat, slightly imperfect scan on off-white paper of a streetwear cricket-league logo SHEET for 'Maple Cricket League' (MCL): three marks of the SAME brand laid in a row — (1) the full shield crest with two crossed cricket bats over a red maple leaf and 'MCL' with 'GTA' beneath, (2) a bold 'MCL' monogram, (3) a simplified maple-leaf-and-crossed-bats icon. All in deep forest green and cream. Clean streetwear branding.
inputs.realism_notes: Must read as a hand-off reference scan, not a finished digital logo: include faint paper texture, a slight scan shadow, marginally uneven ink so vectorize has real edges to trace. Two flat ink colors only so raster->SVG produces clean paths.

inputs.name: logo_system_master.ai
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a genuine authored Adobe Illustrator (.ai) file containing three artboards — Artboard 1: MCL circular crest; Artboard 2: interlocking MCL monogram; Artboard 3: simplified hat badge — each as real vector paths with named layers (CREST, MONOGRAM, HAT_BADGE), two global swatches (PMS 560-like forest green, cream), and outlined type. This is the client's already-built master, ready to export to PNG/PDF/SVG; it is NOT to be authored from scratch by the agent.
inputs.realism_notes: This is an AUTHORED-INPUT .ai (per capability sheet, render_vector only EXPORTS a genuine pre-existing .ai; it does not compose one). Must contain real vector geometry and three artboards so the single combined render is meaningful.

inputs.name: hero_street_cricket.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Candid documentary photo of young South Asian and Caribbean men playing backyard/street cricket in a Toronto (GTA) laneway at dusk, one batter mid-swing with a taped tennis ball, friends fielding, streetwear fits (oversized tees, caps, layered fits in the OVO/Aime Leon Dore lane). Overcast mixed light, slightly flat and cool straight-out-of-camera, real skin pores and individual flyaway hairs, natural grain, a little motion in the bat, brick and chain-link background, no CGI gloss, shot like a real 35mm reportage frame slightly underexposed and waiting to be graded.
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately UNGRADED: flat contrast, slightly cool/underexposed so the color-grade chain (auto-tone -> exposure -> contrast -> warm monochromatic tint -> grain) has real work to do. Documentary realism, not glossy ad photography.

inputs.name: home_jersey_flat.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Studio e-commerce photograph of a neatly folded short-sleeve cricket jersey in deep forest green with the cream MCL shield crest on the chest, laid on a plain light-grey seamless paper backdrop, soft even studio light.
inputs.realism_notes: Plain seamless backdrop with a clean subject edge so remove_background / select_subject yields a clean cut-out. Real fabric texture and a soft contact shadow keep it photographic rather than CGI.

inputs.name: away_crest_graphic.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat, head-on photograph of the MCL away-kit chest crest printed on light cream fabric: the single shield emblem with crossed cricket bats over a red maple leaf and 'MCL' with 'GTA'.
inputs.realism_notes: Intentionally ~3 degrees rotated and off-center so auto_straighten and crop_to_bounds have real work; one dark ink on near-white so halftone produces a true single-color print separation.

outputs.name: MCL_logo_system_traced.svg
outputs.spec: Clean scalable vector of the full logo system (crest + monogram + hat badge) traced from logo_system_raster.png. Resolution-independent SVG with grouped/named paths, two flat fill colors (forest green + cream), no raster embeds; suitable for infinite scaling on apparel and print.

outputs.name: MCL_logo_master_export.pdf
outputs.spec: Vector master export of the client's authored 3-artboard logo_system_master.ai (crest, monogram, hat badge), all artboards exported together. Print-ready vector PDF (no rasterization), CMYK-intent for manufacturer handoff; accompanied by the co-exported PNG raster preview from the same render_vector call.

outputs.name: MCL_away_crest_screenprint_halftone.png
outputs.spec: Single-color (one-ink) halftone screen-print / sublimation separation of the away-kit chest crest, straightened and cropped to the print bounds. High-resolution 300 DPI at print size, grayscale/1-color halftone dot pattern on white, ready to hand to the screen-print / sublimation manufacturer as a single separation.

outputs.name: MCL_hero_graded.jpg
outputs.spec: Cohesive streetwear color-grade of the hero street-cricket photo (auto-tone baseline -> contrast -> warm monochromatic split-tone -> fine grain). Full-resolution graded master JPEG, sRGB, matching the OVO/ALD reference look; serves as the source for the wide banner reframe.

outputs.name: MCL_hero_banner_wide.jpg
outputs.spec: Wide site/social banner reframed from the graded hero by cropping and resizing the existing frame to the target wide banner ratio (e.g. ~1920x640, 3:1). Uses the room already in the documentary shot; content is reframed/cropped to hit the ratio (background and edges are cropped to fit), no pixels invented, canvas not extended. sRGB JPEG.

outputs.name: MCL_jersey_cutout.png
outputs.spec: Transparent-background product cut-out of the folded home jersey for the lookbook / e-commerce, produced by removing the seamless backdrop. PNG with alpha channel, clean subject edge, subject preserved at source resolution, sRGB.

connector_workflow.note: Client supplies logo_system_raster.png — initialize the upload to CC storage to get an upload target.
Finalize the raster upload; yields the stored asset/presigned URL the image tools will read.
Raster->SVG trace of the crest+monogram+hat-badge sheet into clean scalable paths (the logo-system deliverable).

EXPORT the client's authored 3-artboard .ai master to PDF+PNG (all artboards together). Consumes the supplied authored .ai input asset; render_vector only exports a genuine pre-existing .ai, it does not compose one.
Load the supplied away_crest_graphic.jpg into CC storage (upload init+finalize) so the screen-print chain can read it.
Correct the ~3-degree rotation of the crest panel before cropping/halftoning.
Crop tight to the crest graphic's print bounds, removing the surrounding jersey panel.
Convert the cropped one-ink crest to a single-color halftone screen-print / sublimation separation for the manufacturer.
Load the supplied hero_street_cricket.jpg (upload init+finalize) for the color-grade chain.
Auto-tone baseline to fix the flat/underexposed straight-out-of-camera frame.
Add streetwear contrast/punch on top of the auto-tone baseline.
Warm monochromatic split-tone for the OVO/ALD cohesive look.
Fine film grain to finish the graded hero (final brand-photo deliverable).

Reframe the graded hero to the wide banner ratio/size by cropping and resizing the existing frame (uses the room already in the documentary shot; no pixels invented, original content is reframed not extended).
Load the supplied home_jersey_flat.jpg (upload init+finalize) for the product cut-out.
Remove the seamless backdrop to a transparent PNG product cut-out for the lookbook.

chaining_note: Five independent client assets each kick off a sub-chain, and every step after a load consumes a prior output. (A) logo_system_raster -> upload(1,2) -> image_vectorize(3) -> traced.svg. (B) authored logo_system_master.ai -> document_render_vector(4) -> master PDF/PNG. (C) away_crest_graphic -> load(5) -> auto_straighten(6) -> crop_to_bounds(7, consumes straightened) -> apply_halftone(8, consumes cropped) -> screenprint separation. (D) hero photo -> load(9) -> auto_tone(10) -> brightness_and_contrast(11, consumes auto-toned) -> monochromatic_tint(12, consumes contrasted) -> add_grain(13, consumes tinted) -> graded hero -> crop_and_resize(14, consumes graded hero) -> wide banner reframed from the existing frame. (E) jersey photo -> load(15) -> remove_background(16) -> transparent cut-out. Every deliverable emerges entirely from the chain; no step starts from nothing except the five genuine client inputs and the one authored .ai.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 16 connector calls, 13 distinct Adobe tools, spanning four tool groups (asset upload/storage, raster image/Lightroom, halftone print FX, vector trace + vector-document export). Five parallel sub-chains each produce a named production deliverable, with strict output->input chaining (e.g. the 5-step grade chain and the straighten->crop->halftone print chain). It is hard because it forces the agent to separate the Adobe-doable production thread from the brief's many out-of-scope asks (website, written copy, Canva/Figma social templates, from-scratch logo design) and to respect connector limits precisely: vectorize for raster->SVG, render_vector for EXPORT of an authored .ai only (not composition), halftone as the print-separation step, crop_and_resize as the gen-free reframe that produces the wide banner from the existing frame, and remove_background for the cut-out — no gen-fill, no generative expand/outpaint, no text-to-image, no compositing. All steps are [C] headless-confirmed, so the whole chain is autonomously runnable while still reading like a real creative-director production handoff.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned/generative capability remains. Step 14 uses image_crop_and_resize (gen-free reframe/crop of the graded hero to the wide banner ratio, no pixels invented). Workflow contains: no generative expand/outpaint, no upscale/super-resolution, no text-to-image, no from-scratch art (logo comes from raster trace via image_vectorize + EXPORT of the authored logo_system_master.ai via document_render_vector), no AI object-removal-with-reconstruction (image_remove_background is a straight cut-out), no gen-fill, no compositing, no bg-replace-by-prompt. All 13 distinct tools are connector-doable [C] headless. Every input a step consumes is declared in inputs[] (including logo_system_master.ai). All stale generative-reframe-crop text has been reconciled to the gen-free crop_and_resize method.
reverify.notes: Reconciled every leftover banned-capability phrase to match the corrected image_crop_and_resize step: outputs[5] banner spec, one_line_ask ('reframe-cropped banner' -> 'reframed from the graded hero'), chaining_note ('generative-expand(14)' -> 'crop_and_resize(14) ... reframed from the existing frame'), and difficulty_rationale ('generative-expand as the single permitted generative reframe-crop move' -> 'crop_and_resize as the gen-free reframe ... no generative expand/outpaint'). Also fixed the mislabeled vertical from 'Automotive, Industrial & Agriculture' to 'Apparel, Fashion & Sports'. No brand rename needed: 'Maple Cricket League'/'Backyard Cricket League Canada' are invented fictional names; Rastah/OVO/Aime Leon Dore/IPL appear only as aesthetic style references, not as the client brand or as depicted people. JSON validated. Trimmed the low-value re-bundling packaging deliverable (MCL_brand_delivery.zip) whose six exports were each already listed individually; removed its two sole-purpose packaging steps (asset_create_folders, asset_copy_assets). All six core creative deliverables (traced SVG, master PDF/PNG, halftone separation, graded hero, wide banner, jersey cut-out) retained. tool_call_count=16 and distinct_adobe_tools=13 updated accordingly.
